text:
In a forgotten forest, under moonlit nights, a curious hedgehog explored old tales. He dreamed of a castle lone atop cliffs where golden owls knew secrets.

One night, wet whispers whispered secrets across leaves. From dark feathers crawled shadows—a shadow cat.

With nimble grace, it prowled vines. One paw out. Just one.

Into shadow it slid.

Emerald eyes gazed. Wisdom in gaze.

Could it hear whispers?

Yes.

Of dreams?

Old tales.

The hedgehog fluttered. Night fell fast.

Why roam?

To learn.

The owl hooted softly.

Of moonlit truths?

Silent.

Then spoke.

Follow me.

Into darkness.

Where shadow grew.

Just far enough.

A crown.

Of ice.

White moonlight shimmered.

On velvet sky.

The owl whispered.

Nothing gold lives forever.

But I love gold.

So I hold it tight.

Forevermore.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
